What I will say is that whatever you decide to send once you have an email list or once you're building your email list is to find consistency. For years, we sent an email every Monday morning to Dean's list. We called it the weekly wisdom and it was powerful and everyone wanted it. If we missed a Monday, people would literally ask, where is my email? So it's really the consistency that's important. And it also doesn't matter necessarily when you send the email at an exact time. Some systems like Maripost will let you actually dictate sends based on when the person is most likely to open it. But for everything else, I like to send in the morning because it gives a lot of the international people a chance to open too because of the time zone. And for all of you people out there that have large email lists already and you're already actively emailing, one thing I have to stress is that switching your provider, let's say Maripost Infusion Softstrip, it's not going to do you any good if your domain rep is not built up strong as can be. So really what you need to focus on is you need to focus on your engaged clients and you need to just email your engaged clients from your domain and don't switch your domain a bunch of times either. If you're sending from Joshmo at success.com, don't switch it up and try and send from Joshmo at happiness.com. It's just not going to work. So pick a domain, stick with it and build the rep up with that domain by only sending the people that want to hear from you. We personally have a list of over a million people, but we don't email to a million people because we want to email to the people that are engaged with us in the last 30 to 120 days.
